---
title: "IML_XGB"
author: "Philip Nordfang"
date: "2026-05-19"
output: html_document
---

```{r}
library(mlr3)
library(mlr3learners)
library(mlr3tuning)
library(mlr3mbo)
library(mlr3pipelines)
library(paradox)
library(future)
library(tidyverse)
library(forcats)
library(mlr3extralearners)
library(OpenML)
library(mgcv)
library(kknn)
```

###### DATA HANDLING ######
```{r}
MPL_data = freMPL
```

```{r}
summary(MPL_data)
```


```{r}
hist(MPL_data$ClaimAmount, main ="Claim amount distribution",
    xlab ="Claim Amount",
    col ="blue")
```

```{r}
MPL_data <- subset(MPL_data, select = -ClaimInd)
MPL_data$RecordEnd[is.na(MPL_data$RecordEnd)] <- as.Date("2005-01-01")
```

```{r}
# Creating a new data set where few observations are lumped together as "other" to avoid problems with test-observations that are not not seen in the training data
MPL_data_lump <- MPL_data

factor_cols <- names(MPL_data_lump)[sapply(MPL_data_lump, function(x) is.factor(x) || is.character(x))]

for (col in factor_cols) {
  MPL_data_lump[[col]] <- as.factor(MPL_data_lump[[col]])
  MPL_data_lump[[col]] <- fct_lump_min(MPL_data_lump[[col]], min = 10)
  MPL_data_lump[[col]] <- droplevels(MPL_data_lump[[col]])
}
```

```{r}
unique(MPL_data_lump$SocioCateg)
```

### convert categorical variables to factors
```{r}
categorical_vars <- c("VehAge", "Gender", "MariStat", "SocioCateg", "VehUsage", "VehBody", "VehPrice", "VehEngine", "VehEnergy", "VehMaxSpeed", "VehClass", "Garage") # VehMax Speed should perhaps be converted to a numeric variable by taking eg the max number in the observations given interval
MPL_data_lump[categorical_vars] <- lapply(MPL_data_lump[categorical_vars], factor)
```

##### DEFINING XGBoost MODEL #####

```{r}
task_xgb <- TaskRegr$new(
  id = "claim_amount",
  backend = MPL_data_lump,
  target = "ClaimAmount"
)
```

```{r}
set.seed(2026)

split <- partition(task_xgb, ratio = 0.8)

train_set <- split$train
test_set  <- split$test
```

```{r}
learner_xgb <- as_learner(
  
  po("encode", method = "one-hot") %>>%
    
    lrn("regr.xgboost",
        objective = "reg:squarederror",
        eval_metric = "rmse"
    )
)
```

```{r}
search_space <- ps(
  regr.xgboost.eta = p_dbl(0.01, 0.3),
  regr.xgboost.max_depth = p_int(3, 10),
  regr.xgboost.subsample = p_dbl(0.5, 1),
  regr.xgboost.colsample_bytree = p_dbl(0.5, 1),
  regr.xgboost.min_child_weight = p_int(1, 10),
  regr.xgboost.nrounds = p_int(100, 1000)
)
```

```{r}
resampling <- rsmp("cv", folds = 5)
```

```{r}
tuner <- tnr("random_search")
```

```{r}
terminator <- trm("evals", n_evals = 50)
```

```{r}
instance <- TuningInstanceBatchSingleCrit$new(
  task = task_xgb,
  learner = learner_xgb,
  resampling = resampling,
  measure = msr("regr.rmse"),
  search_space = search_space,
  terminator = terminator
)
```

```{r}
plan(multisession)
tuner$optimize(instance)
```


```{r}
learner_xgb$param_set$values <- instance$result_learner_param_vals
```

```{r}
learner_xgb$train(task_xgb, row_ids = train_set)
```

```{r}
pred_xgb <- learner_xgb$predict(task_xgb, row_ids = test_set)

pred_xgb$score(list(
  msr("regr.rmse"),
  msr("regr.mae"),
  msr("regr.rsq")
))
```


#### BASELINE MODEL FOR COMPARISON ######

```{r}
baseline <- lrn("regr.featureless")
```

```{r}
baseline$train(task_knn, row_ids = split_knn$train)
```

```{r}
pred_baseline <- baseline$predict(task_knn, row_ids = split_knn$test)
```

```{r}
pred_baseline$score(list(
  msr("regr.rmse"),
  msr("regr.mae"),
  msr("regr.rsq")
))
```
